message:
feat(cli): redact agent mail send (bd-3a7yx)

Adds redaction preflight for mail overseer send + tests; resets redaction flags in test helper.

 {"id":"bd-39uo","title":"Task: Implement interactive Bubble Tea wizard","description":"## Purpose
Create an interactive setup wizard for non-technical users using Bubble Tea TUI framework.

## Wizard Flow
1. Project type selection (Go/Python/Node/Rust/Other)
2. Agent count configuration
3. Integration toggles (Agent Mail, CASS, CM)
4. File reservation auto-enable toggle
5. Confirmation and summary

## UI Components
- Selection list for project type
- Number input for agent count
- Toggle/checkbox for integrations
- Progress indicator during setup
- Summary display at end

## Acceptance Criteria
- Wizard runs by default, --non-interactive skips it
- All options configurable through wizard
- Escape key exits cleanly
- Summary shows all chosen options before confirming

## Technical Notes
Use existing Bubble Tea patterns from internal/tui/
Match dashboard styling (Catppuccin theme)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:31:55.603636143Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T09:07:55.578235604Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T09:07:55.578151726Z","close_reason":"Interactive Bubble Tea wizard already implemented in init_wizard.go","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-39uo","depends_on_id":"bd-38gl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:28Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3a6a7","title":"Task: Implement time-boxed ensemble execution","description":"## Implementation
Add timeout-aware execution to ensemble runs:

1. Scheduler integration (prefer extending existing manager/scheduler code):
   - Apply deadline from `--timeout`
   - Order modes by priority metadata + expected value/cost
   - Track elapsed time; stop launching new modes once budget is consumed

2. Graceful degradation:
   - Allow in-flight modes to finish
   - Skip remaining modes and record reason
   - Synthesize with available outputs

3. User output:
   - Report `completed N/M modes (timeout)`
   - Provide resume hint or suggestion for longer timeout

## Logging
- Log deadline, ordering decisions, skipped modes + reasons

## Tests
- Unit: ordering + cutoff logic
- Integration: timeout triggers partial synthesis
- E2E: timeout + partial output path

## Dependencies
- EnsembleManager (bd-lp5h4)
- Budget/Resource Control (bd-1dt84)
- Mode priority metadata (bd-3806s)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:40:46.778814883Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T21:56:41.818751860Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3a6a7","depends_on_id":"bd-1dt84","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:28Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3a6a7","depends_on_id":"bd-2nknt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:28Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3a6a7","depends_on_id":"bd-3806s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:28Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3a6a7","depends_on_id":"bd-lp5h4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:28Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":24,"issue_id":"bd-3a6a7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Note: Mode prioritization within time budget uses estimated RUNTIME (seconds), not token cost. Mode runtime can be estimated from historical data or complexity heuristics. This is intentionally separate from Token Budget Advisor which focuses on COST.","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:49:43Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-3a7yx","title":"Task: Apply redaction to ntm mail send","description":"## Scope
Before sending messages into Agent Mail threads/inboxes, scan and optionally redact/block sensitive text.

## Why
Agent Mail persists messages in git-auditable archives. Accidentally sending secrets creates a hard-to-remove leak.

## Behavior
- Apply same `--redact`/`--allow-secret` logic as `ntm send`.
- For `warn` mode, warn loudly (human) and include a redaction summary.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Messages are never persisted with raw secrets when redact/block is enabled.
- Robot mode (if/when `--robot-mail-send` exists) uses stable error codes.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:22:34.345700980Z","created_by":"OrangeWolf","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:22:34.365761234Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3a7yx","depends_on_id":"bd-1sjqs","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:22:34.357755941Z","created_by":"OrangeWolf"},{"issue_id":"bd-3a7yx","depends_on_id":"bd-20xv1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:22:34.365738562Z","created_by":"OrangeWolf"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3acv","title":"Task: Create Archiver background service","description":"## Purpose
Implement background archiver that captures agent output for CASS indexing.

## Archiver Struct
type Archiver struct {
    sessionName string
    outputDir   string
    interval    time.Duration
    lastOffsets map[string]int // pane -> last archived line
}

## Archive Loop
func (a *Archiver) Run(ctx context.Context) {
    ticker := time.NewTicker(a.interval)
    for {
        select {
        case <-ctx.Done():
            a.flush() // Final archive on shutdown
            return
        case <-ticker.C:
            a.archiveNewContent()
        }
    }
}

## Output Format (CASS-compatible JSONL)
{"session": "myproject", "pane": "cc_1", "agent": "claude", "timestamp": "2026-01-19T10:30:00Z", "content": "..."}

## Acceptance Criteria
- Background archiving with configurable interval
- Incremental capture (no duplicates)
- Flush on shutdown
- CASS-compatible output format

## Technical Notes
Use goroutine with context
Store archive in .ntm/archive/","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-3ah0k","title":"Implement interrupt_test probe method","description":"Implement interrupt_test probe method.

## Method

Send Ctrl-C and observe response. More aggressive but definitive.

## When to Use

- When keystroke_echo is ambiguous
- With --aggressive flag
- As confirmation before kill

## Implementation

```go
func probeInterruptTest(session string, pane int, timeout time.Duration) ProbeResult {
    baseline := capturePaneContent(session, pane)
    
    // Send single Ctrl-C (not the double-tap that exits)
    exec.Command("/usr/bin/tmux", "send-keys", "-t", target, "C-c").Run()
    
    // Wait for response
    deadline := time.Now().Add(timeout)
    for time.Now().Before(deadline) {
        current := capturePaneContent(session, pane)
        if current != baseline {
            // Check if it's showing interrupt response
            return ProbeResult{
                Responsive: true,
                Method: "interrupt_test",
            }
        }
        time.Sleep(100 * time.Millisecond)
    }
    
    return ProbeResult{Responsive: false}
}
```

## Warning

This method may:
- Interrupt ongoing work
- Cause loss of in-progress output
- Not be recoverable

Document clearly in output that this is disruptive.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:33:10.174682714Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T18:47:36.046096410Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T18:47:36.046033962Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ah0k","depends_on_id":"bd-1cu1f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:28Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ah0k","depends_on_id":"bd-58w0z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:28Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ah0k","depends_on_id":"bd-ok7rj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:28Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-p6quz","title":"Feature: Robot API - JSON interface for ensemble automation","description":"# Feature: Robot API

## Purpose
Expose ensemble functionality through JSON API for AI-to-AI orchestration and automation pipelines. Follows existing NTM robot mode patterns.

## Scope

### Robot Commands

**--robot-ensemble=SESSION**
Get ensemble state for a session.
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "timestamp": "2026-01-23T10:30:00Z",
  "ensemble": {
    "session": "myproject",
    "question": "What architectural problems exist?",
    "preset": "architecture-review",
    "status": "active",
    "started_at": "2026-01-23T10:25:00Z",
    "modes": [
      {"id": "abductive", "code": "B13", "tier": "advanced", "pane": "myproject__cc_1", "agent_type": "claude", "status": "done"}
    ],
    "synthesis": {"strategy": "deliberative", "status": "not_started"}
  },
  "summary": {"total_modes": 5, "completed": 2, "working": 2, "pending": 1}
}
```

**--robot-ensemble-spawn=SESSION**
Create new ensemble session.
```bash
ntm --robot-ensemble-spawn=myproject \
  --preset=architecture-review \
  --question='What problems exist?' \
  --agents=cc=3,cod=2

ntm --robot-ensemble-spawn=myproject \
  --modes=A1,B13,F43 \
  --allow-advanced=true \
  --question='...'
```

**--robot-ensemble-synthesize=SESSION**
Trigger synthesis.
```bash
ntm --robot-ensemble-synthesize=myproject \
  --strategy=deliberative \
  --format=markdown
```
Returns synthesis output metadata or status.

**--robot-ensemble-modes**
List available modes.
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "modes": [
    {"id": "abductive", "code": "B13", "tier": "advanced", "name": "Abductive Reasoning", "category": {"code": "B", "name": "Ampliative"}}
  ],
  "categories": [{"code": "A", "name": "Formal", "mode_count": 8}],
  "default_tier": "core",
  "total_modes": 80,
  "core_modes": 28,
  "advanced_modes": 52,
  "experimental_modes": 0
}
```

**--robot-ensemble-presets**
List available ensembles (presets).
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "presets": [
    {"name": "architecture-review", "display": "Architecture Review", "description": "...", "modes": [{"id":"systems-thinking","code":"F43"}], "synthesis": {"strategy": "deliberative"}, "budget": {"total": 30000}, "allow_advanced": false, "tags": ["architecture"]}
  ]
}
```

### Modifiers (Unprefixed)
- **Spawn:** `--question`, `--preset`, `--modes`, `--agents`, `--assignment`, `--allow-advanced`, `--budget-total`, `--budget-per-agent`, `--no-cache`, `--no-questions`, `--project`
- **Modes list:** `--category`, `--tier`, `--limit`, `--offset`
- **Synthesize:** `--strategy`, `--format`, `--output`, `--force`

### Strategy Set
Uses canonical set from bd-2ztw9 (manual, adversarial, consensus, creative, analytical, deliberative, prioritized, dialectical, meta-reasoning, voting, argumentation-graph).

### Exit Codes
- 0: Success
- 1: Error (with error_code in JSON)
- 2: Feature unavailable

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All robot commands return valid JSON
- [ ] Follows existing robot envelope pattern (success/timestamp, optional error fields on failure)
- [ ] Exit codes are consistent
- [ ] Arrays initialized to [] not nil
- [ ] Error codes are documented
- [ ] _agent_hints included where helpful

## Technical Notes
- Follow patterns in internal/robot/
- Add to capabilities.go for discoverability
- Ensure envelope compliance (see envelope_test.go)
- Modifiers are **unprefixed** per docs/robot-api-design.md","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:26:15.832578580Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T20:12:58.509021100Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-p6quz","depends_on_id":"bd-12u4u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-p6quz","depends_on_id":"bd-49fb9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-pjjk7","title":"EPIC: Canonical Assignment Flow (ntm assign + spawn --assign)","description":"## Goal
Reduce user confusion by providing **one** canonical assignment flow and **one** convenience entry point.

## Decision
- **Keep**: `ntm assign` as the only assignment command.
- **Keep**: `ntm spawn --assign` as the only convenience entry (spawn + assign).
- **Expose**: `--strategy=balanced|speed|quality|dependency|round-robin` on `ntm assign`.
- **Drop/Merge**: `ntm swarm`, `ntm send --distribute`, YAML swarm config, and from‑file assignments.

## Why
Multiple entry points doing the same thing (assign work to agents) multiplies docs, tests, and code paths and will confuse users. The project benefits from a single, well‑designed path.

## Scope
- Keep existing strategies (`balanced`, `speed`, `quality`, `dependency`) intact and documented.
- Add `round‑robin` as an **optional** additional strategy (no renaming of existing ones).
- Implement assignment tracking + reporting so `balanced` can be truly load‑aware.
- Implement `ntm spawn --assign` as a thin wrapper over `ntm assign`.
- Update docs/help to emphasize canonical flow.

## Non‑Goals
- No new orchestration commands outside `assign` + `spawn --assign`.
- No YAML swarm config or additional CLI entry points.

## Success Criteria
- `ntm assign` is the only path for distributing work.
- `ntm spawn --assign` works end‑to‑end with clear output.
- Redundant commands are closed/dropped.
- Strategy list in docs/help matches code.","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-pndtv","title":"Unit Tests: AutoRespawner kill sequences and respawn logic","description":"## Purpose
Comprehensive unit tests for AutoRespawner covering agent-specific kill sequences, respawn cycles, and integration with AccountRotator.

## Test Structure

```go
// internal/swarm/auto_respawner_test.go
package swarm

func TestAutoRespawner_KillSequences(t *testing.T) {
    tests := []struct {
        name      string
        agentType string
        expectedKeys []string
    }{
        {"claude_double_ctrl_c", "cc", []string{"\x03", "\x03"}},
        {"codex_exit_command", "cod", []string{"/exit\n"}},
        {"gemini_escape_ctrl_c", "gmi", []string{"\x1b", "\x03"}},
    }
    
    for _, tt := range tests {
        t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
            mockPaneSpawner := &MockPaneSpawner{t: t}
            respawner := &AutoRespawner{PaneSpawner: mockPaneSpawner}
            
            t.Logf("[TEST] Testing kill sequence for agent type: %s", tt.agentType)
            
            err := respawner.killAgent("test_session:1.0", tt.agentType)
            require.NoError(t, err)
            
            t.Logf("[TEST] Keys sent: %v", mockPaneSpawner.keysSent)
            assert.Equal(t, tt.expectedKeys, mockPaneSpawner.keysSent)
        })
    }
}

func TestAutoRespawner_ForceKillFallback(t *testing.T) {
    mockPaneSpawner := &MockPaneSpawner{
        panePIDs: map[string]int{"test_session:1.0": 12345},
        exitAfter: 3 * time.Second,
    }
    cfg := &config.SwarmConfig{GracefulTimeout: 1 * time.Second}
    respawner := NewAutoRespawner(cfg, mockPaneSpawner, nil, nil)
    
    t.Log("[TEST] Testing force kill fallback")
    err := respawner.killWithFallback(ctx, "test_session:1.0", "cc")
    require.NoError(t, err)
    assert.True(t, mockPaneSpawner.forceKillCalled)
}

func TestAutoRespawner_MaxRetriesAndCooldown(t *testing.T) {
    cfg := &config.SwarmConfig{MaxRespawnsPerPane: 3, RespawnCooldown: 100*time.Millisecond}
    respawner := NewAutoRespawner(cfg, &MockPaneSpawner{}, nil, nil)
    
    pane := "test_session:1.0"
    for i := 0; i < 3; i++ {
        respawner.recordRespawn(pane)
    }
    assert.False(t, respawner.canRespawn(pane), "Should NOT allow after max retries")
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Test kill sequences for all 3 agent types (cc, cod, gmi)
- [ ] Test force kill fallback when graceful fails
- [ ] Test max retries limit enforcement
- [ ] Test cooldown period enforcement
- [ ] Mock PaneSpawner, AccountRotator, PromptInjector
- [ ] Detailed test logging with [TEST] prefix","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:32:06.873537077Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:18:24.457935190Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T18:18:24.457848850Z","close_reason":"Added AutoRespawner kill/force-kill tests + tmux mock interface; go test ./internal/swarm -run AutoRespawner","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-pndtv","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-pqwk","title":"Support partial/prefix session names for disambiguation","description":"Support partial/prefix session names for disambiguation.

## Problem Being Solved

Currently you need to type full session name like `vnt destructive_command_guard`. Should support prefix matching.

## Behavior

- If prefix matches exactly one session: use it
- If prefix matches multiple sessions: show matching options, ask user to be more specific
- If prefix matches zero sessions: show error with available sessions

## Examples

```bash
# Currently required
ntm view destructive_command_guard

# Desired
ntm view destr  # works if only one session starts with 'destr'
vnt destr       # same with alias

# Ambiguous
ntm view my
# Error: 'my' matches multiple sessions: my_project, my_test
# Please be more specific.
```

## Implementation

Update session name resolution in all commands:
- ntm view SESSION
- ntm kill SESSION
- ntm send SESSION
- ntm health SESSION
- etc.

## Robot Mode API
Robot mode requires exact names (no fuzzy matching).

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Prefix matching works for all commands taking SESSION arg
2. Exact match preferred over prefix match
3. Multiple matches show list and error
4. Zero matches show available sessions
5. Robot mode requires exact names
6. Works with vnt alias

## Unit Tests (80% coverage target)
- internal/session/resolve_test.go
- Test exact match priority
- Test unique prefix resolution
- Test ambiguous prefix error

## E2E Test Script
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# [E2E-PREFIX] Session Prefix Resolution Tests
LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-PREFIX]"
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Testing prefix session names"
ntm spawn test_session_one --cc=1 --no-prompt
ntm spawn test_session_two --cc=1 --no-prompt
ntm view test_session_o && echo "Prefix worked"
ntm kill test_session_one --force
ntm kill test_session_two --force
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Prefix test complete"
```","acceptance_criteria":"1. Partial session names work for disambiguation
2. Prefix matching finds unique session
3. Multiple matches returns error with suggestions
4. Works with all session-accepting commands
5. Unit tests for matching logic
6. E2E test with multiple sessions","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

+{"id":"bd-pqwk","title":"Support partial/prefix session names for disambiguation","description":"Support partial/prefix session names for disambiguation.

## Problem Being Solved

Currently you need to type full session name like `vnt destructive_command_guard`. Should support prefix matching.

## Behavior

- If prefix matches exactly one session: use it
- If prefix matches multiple sessions: show matching options, ask user to be more specific
- If prefix matches zero sessions: show error with available sessions

## Examples

```bash
# Currently required
ntm view destructive_command_guard

# Desired
ntm view destr  # works if only one session starts with 'destr'
vnt destr       # same with alias

# Ambiguous
ntm view my
# Error: 'my' matches multiple sessions: my_project, my_test
# Please be more specific.
```

## Implementation

Update session name resolution in all commands:
- ntm view SESSION
- ntm kill SESSION
- ntm send SESSION
- ntm health SESSION
- etc.

## Robot Mode API
Robot mode requires exact names (no fuzzy matching).

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Prefix matching works for all commands taking SESSION arg
2. Exact match preferred over prefix match
3. Multiple matches show list and error
4. Zero matches show available sessions
5. Robot mode requires exact names
6. Works with vnt alias

## Unit Tests (80% coverage target)
- internal/session/resolve_test.go
- Test exact match priority
- Test unique prefix resolution
- Test ambiguous prefix error

## E2E Test Script
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# [E2E-PREFIX] Session Prefix Resolution Tests
LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-PREFIX]"
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Testing prefix session names"
ntm spawn test_session_one --cc=1 --no-prompt
ntm spawn test_session_two --cc=1 --no-prompt
ntm view test_session_o && echo "Prefix worked"
ntm kill test_session_one --force
ntm kill test_session_two --force
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Prefix test complete"
```","acceptance_criteria":"1. Partial session names work for disambiguation
2. Prefix matching finds unique session
3. Multiple matches returns error with suggestions
4. Works with all session-accepting commands
5. Unit tests for matching logic
6. E2E test with multiple sessions","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-ptu2","title":"Task: Add event markers and details overlay","description":"## Overview
Add discrete event markers on the timeline with hover/select details.

## Deliverables
1. Event marker types:
   - Prompt sent (▶): When user sends prompt to agent
   - Completion (✓): When agent finishes a task
   - Error (✗): When error detected
   - Start/Stop (◆): Session lifecycle events

2. Marker rendering:
   - Small icons above timeline bar at event time
   - Different colors for different event types
   - Clustered markers when events too close together

3. Details overlay:
   - Show popup when marker selected
   - Include: timestamp, event type, details
   - For prompts: first 50 chars of message
   - For errors: error message

4. Interaction:
   - Arrow keys to navigate between markers
   - Enter to expand marker details
   - Esc to close overlay

## Implementation Notes
- Use unicode symbols for markers
- Popup positioning to avoid edge overflow
- Cache marker positions for efficient navigation

## Acceptance Criteria
- Markers appear at correct times
- Different event types visually distinct
- Details overlay shows on selection
- Navigation between markers works
- Unit tests for marker placement","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:28:03.496917374Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T04:20:48.617338102Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T04:20:48.617205812Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ptu2","depends_on_id":"bd-3h0n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-ptu2","depends_on_id":"bd-f815","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}
